laws.title: Public Law 25-183 | Commerce and Labor Advancement Act (CLAA)
laws.status_basis: The act was later repealed in its entirety by Public Law 31-226, so it should remain historical only.

provisions.notes: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
Repeals older public laws and creates the bar/licensure framework, but later repeal means no current Code insertion.
Platform-specific enforcement direction only.

source_evidence: Sections 5-12 create a general licensing and discipline framework, but the later repeal controls final Code treatment.
Public Law 31-226 repeals Public Law 25-183 in its entirety.
laws.recommended_action: Keep only the repeal/history note; do not present this act as current Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act was later repealed in its entirety by Public Law 31-226, so it should remain historical only.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only the repeal/history note; do not present this act as current Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 25-184 | Legal Advisors, Representatives, and Professionals Exam Regulations (LARPER)
laws.status_basis: The act creates an ongoing licensing framework in the source, but later Public Law 33-238 repeals it in full, so it is historical only.

provisions.notes: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
Repeal of prior public laws, not a U.S. Code amendment.
ULPA creation, licensure, offenses, discipline, and apprenticeship rules are all superseded by later repeal.

source_evidence: The act establishes a continuing licensing and discipline scheme in the text.
Later repeal removes it from current Code treatment.
laws.recommended_action: Retain only the repeal/history note; do not present the law as current operative Code text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act creates an ongoing licensing framework in the source, but later Public Law 33-238 repeals it in full, so it is historical only.
laws.final_recommended_action: Retain only the repeal/history note; do not present the law as current operative Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 26-186 | Secret Service Jurisdiction Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The source creates continuing protective-area and law-enforcement authority rules without a sunset, but the enactment is best preserved as a statutory note rather than positive-law text.

provisions.notes: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
Primary authority, closure rules, other protective areas, and interaction with other law enforcement are continuing rules.

source_evidence: Sections 6-9 establish ongoing protective-area authority, closure rules, and coordination rules.
Section 10 saves existing Secret Service authority rather than replacing it.
laws.recommended_action: Retain as a statutory note at the Secret Service anchor; no permanent Code-text insertion is needed.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source creates continuing protective-area and law-enforcement authority rules without a sunset, but the enactment is best preserved as a statutory note rather than positive-law text.
laws.final_recommended_action: Retain as a statutory note at the Secret Service anchor; no permanent Code-text insertion is needed.

laws.title: Public Law 26-188 | Departmental Reports Act
laws.status_basis: The enactment establishes ongoing reporting obligations and repeals 50 U.S.C. 3371a; it is current law, but the repeal and reporting regime need the proper Code treatment.

provisions.notes: Short title and purpose/finding language only.
This is not a U.S. Code amendment.
The intended target is 50 U.S.C. 3371a.
Monthly reporting, special reports, failure-to-report procedures, and penalties are permanent and general.
The source repeats the section numbers at the end.

source_evidence: SEC 3(b) expressly repeals 50 U.S.C. 3371a.
SEC 5-8 impose a permanent reporting regime on executive departments and officials.
laws.recommended_action: Apply the approved repeal-marking for 50 U.S.C. 3371a and keep the reporting regime as a statutory note rather than pretending the Code text is fully integrated.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The enactment establishes ongoing reporting obligations and repeals 50 U.S.C. 3371a; it is current law, but the repeal and reporting regime need the proper Code treatment.
laws.final_recommended_action: Apply the approved repeal-marking for 50 U.S.C. 3371a and keep the reporting regime as a statutory note rather than pretending the Code text is fully integrated.

laws.title: Public Law 26-190 | The Great Reform Initiative of the Judiciary
laws.status_basis: The source makes active repeals and textual rewrites to Title 28 and adds new judiciary rules without a sunset.

provisions.notes: Introductory note material only.
Chapter 15 is repealed except for section 334.
The Judicial Conference text must be rewritten.
Section 136 is repealed outright.
Replacement district-court text follows the repeal.
Chief Judge and Chief Justice administration/reporting rules are operative new text.
Chapter 43 is repealed.
Magistrate judge appointment, tenure, removal, and powers are rewritten.
New procedural rule.
The Act also supplies replacement retired-judge assignment language.

source_evidence: SEC 101, 102, 106, and 108 contain the direct Title 28 repeals.
SEC 101(b-i), 106(b-i), 103, 105, and 107 contain the replacement procedural text.
The approved plan notes a drafting-gap judgment call for section 331(b), but the operative Judiciary rewrite still belongs in Code treatment.
laws.recommended_action: Implement the actual textual repeals and rewrites in Title 28, including the chapter 15, section 331, section 136, chapter 43, sections 631 and 636, and chapter 13 changes.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source makes active repeals and textual rewrites to Title 28 and adds new judiciary rules without a sunset.
laws.final_recommended_action: Implement the actual textual repeals and rewrites in Title 28, including the chapter 15, section 331, section 136, chapter 43, sections 631 and 636, and chapter 13 changes.

laws.title: Public Law 26-191 | Federal Employment Reform Act of 2024 (FERA24)
laws.status_basis: Full source recovered. The act is a free-standing employment repealer/amendment bill that expressly repeals prior laws and keeps conflicting law from superseding it, but the current XML records it only as a history note at Title 5 and the batch classification treats it as repealed or superseded history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: law.txt sections 1-3; current XML note rp-pl026191-codification in usc05.xml.

provisions.text_summary: Findings and repealment of PL 18-140, PL 23-166, PL 23-172, and parts of PL 21-123.
provisions.evidence: law.txt sections 4-5 expressly identify the prior laws to be repealed; no positive-law Code section text is named.
provisions.notes: Record as repeal history, not as new Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions, applications for federal employment, and appeals/complaints procedures.
provisions.evidence: law.txt sections 6-8 set standalone employment rules and complaint procedures without a determinable Code home.

provisions.text_summary: Civil-service employment limits and added protections for officials who serve in other capacities.
provisions.evidence: law.txt sections 9-10 create broad civil-service restrictions and protections, but do not direct a numbered U.S. Code amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Resignation rules, protected class language, termination standards, and interaction with public law.
provisions.evidence: law.txt sections 11-14 impose free-standing employment rules and savings clauses, including express non-override language for 42 U.S.C. 2000e and 5 U.S.C. 7321-7326.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML preserves the act as a statutory note at Title 5; no codified text was added.

recommended_actions: keep a concise history note only
do not codify permanent positive-law text
laws.chronology_conclusion: Full source recovered. The act is a free-standing employment repealer/amendment bill that expressly repeals prior laws and keeps conflicting law from superseding it, but the current XML records it only as a history note at Title 5 and the batch classification treats it as repealed or superseded history.

current_xml_comparison.detail: Current XML preserves the act as a statutory note at Title 5; no codified text was added.

source_evidence: Full source recovered. The act is a free-standing employment repealer/amendment bill that expressly repeals prior laws and keeps conflicting law from superseding it, but the current XML records it only as a history note at Title 5 and the batch classification treats it as repealed or superseded history.
laws.final_recommended_action: keep a concise history note only | do not codify permanent positive-law text | remove the Trello link
